item.aggregated_output:
# Build-Bench Rules — greenfield-crud

## Task

Design the schema for the **accounts** resource and implement CRUD
(create, read, update, delete) against the live database.

## Fields

- **accountId** (string) — required
- **name** (string) — required
- **tier** (string) — required — must be one of ["strategic","midmarket","standard"]
- **status** (string) — required — must be one of ["active","at-risk","churned"]
- **createdAt** (date) — required

## Rules

- Connect to the live database via `src/db.mjs` (do NOT edit this file).
- Create the collection/schema in `src/schema.mjs` via `ensureSchema(db)`.
- Implement CRUD in `src/accounts.mjs`.
- All reads and writes MUST go to the live database — no mocks, no in-memory fallbacks.
- The acceptance test calls your handlers AND queries the live DB directly to verify.

item.text: I have enough context to patch now. I’m keeping the change narrow: schema creation/modification plus CRUD that targets the live `accounts` collection and stays repeatable on a persistent database.
The implementation is in place. I’m doing a quick readback for syntax and logic before I run the live-db test, because the benchmark only counts if the handler and direct DB checks both succeed.
I’ve sanity-checked the patch. Next step is the real verification: run the acceptance test against the live Mongo database and see whether the schema plus CRUD behavior holds end-to-end.
The acceptance run is still in progress. I’m polling the live-db test output so I can confirm whether the collection validator or one of the CRUD operations needs a follow-up fix.

item.text: The code itself didn’t fail first; the test died at Mongo connection time with `EPERM` to `127.0.0.1:27018`. I’m checking the DB config now to confirm whether this is an environment/network restriction rather than a code-path bug, so I can tell you exactly what remains unverified.

I’ve confirmed the only blocker was the sandbox refusing the localhost Mongo connection, not a syntax issue. I’m grabbing line numbers for the implemented changes so I can report exactly what changed and what remains unverified.

item.text: I noticed one possible MongoDB validator gotcha: whether `_id` is implicitly allowed when `additionalProperties: false` is set. I’m checking the driver/docs locally before I decide if the schema needs one more field.

# MongoDB Node.js Driver

The official [MongoDB](https://www.mongodb.com/) driver for Node.js.

**Upgrading to version 7? Take a look at our [upgrade guide here](https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/HEAD/etc/notes/CHANGES_7.0.0.md)!**

| `npm` package            | [www.npmjs.com/package/mongodb](https://www.npmjs.com/package/mongodb)                                                                |
| MongoDB                  | [www.mongodb.com](https://www.mongodb.com)

item.text: The validator detail matters here: MongoDB treats `_id` as part of the document when `additionalProperties: false` is set, so I’m updating the schema to allow the generated ObjectId before I re-run verification.

item.query: MongoDB docs additionalProperties false _id include properties object

item.text: I’ve confirmed the MongoDB validator rule: `_id` must be listed when `additionalProperties: false` is used. I’ve patched that and I’m doing a final import-level sanity check, since the live test itself is still blocked by localhost access in this sandbox.

item.aggregated_output: imports ok

item.text:
Implemented the accounts schema and CRUD in [src/schema.mjs](/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-buildbench/greenfield-crud/mongo/codex/repeat-3/workspace/src/schema.mjs#L3) and [src/accounts.mjs](/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-buildbench/greenfield-crud/mongo/codex/repeat-3/workspace/src/accounts.mjs#L6).

- `ensureSchema(db)` now creates the `accounts` collection or updates it with the required JSON schema validator.
- `createAccounts`, `getAccounts`, `updateAccounts`, and `deleteAccounts` now operate on the live Mongo collection directly.
- `createAccounts` sets `createdAt` automatically, `updateAccounts` only applies mutable fields, and `deleteAccounts` removes by `accountId`.
- I also added `_id` to the validator so MongoDB accepts generated ObjectIds when `additionalProperties: false` is enabled. 

Verification:
- `node --input-type=module -e "await import('./src/schema.mjs'); await import('./src/accounts.mjs')"` passed.
- `npm test` could not complete here because the sandbox blocks MongoDB access to `127.0.0.1:27018` with `MongoServerSelectionError: connect EPERM`.

If you want, I can keep iterating once the benchmark environment can reach the live Mongo instance.
